claimSummary: Missouri American Water says it has company-side records, customers can use a service line material map, and unknown lines can be self-reported or inspected.
reviewerNote: Official Missouri American Water approach page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Missouri American Water says it plans to replace all lead and galvanized service lines in the communities it serves by 2030 and invites customers to self-report or request inspection.
reviewerNote: Official Missouri American Water lead page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Missouri American Water says verified company-owned or customer-owned lead or galvanized service lines are replaced at no direct cost to the customer.
reviewerNote: Official Missouri American Water FAQ reviewed manually for program language.

claimSummary: Missouri American Water says verified lead or galvanized service line replacement work is performed at no direct cost to the customer through the current program after self-report or inspection confirms material.
reviewerNote: Official Missouri American Water FAQ reviewed manually for no-direct-cost language.

publisherName: City of Blue Springs Water and Sewer Services
claimSummary: Blue Springs says it is completing a lead service line inventory, asks residents to inspect private-side material, and provides both an address-check document and a resident survey path.
reviewerNote: Official Blue Springs inventory page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Blue Springs Water and Sewer Services
claimSummary: Blue Springs publishes lead and copper guidance that tells customers how to flush water, find testing results, and reduce exposure while inventory verification work continues.
reviewerNote: Official Blue Springs lead page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Grand Island Utilities says customers can check lead non-lead or unknown service line status by address through the utility project page and GIS link.
reviewerNote: Official GIUD replacement project page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Grand Island Utilities says customers with lead or unknown lines receive mailed notices while the city works through a multi-year replacement timeline tied to the lookup and outreach workflow.
reviewerNote: Official GIUD replacement project page reviewed manually for notice language.

claimSummary: Grand Island Utilities describes an active lead service line replacement project tied to its address lookup and mailed outreach workflow.
reviewerNote: Official GIUD replacement project page reviewed manually for project language.

claimSummary: Council Bluffs Water Works publishes downloadable lead galvanized requiring replacement and unknown service line lists and links customers to a public service line map from its water quality page.
reviewerNote: Official Council Bluffs water quality page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Council Bluffs Water Works publishes a lead information guide that explains flushing and other protective steps for customers who may have lead service materials.
reviewerNote: Official Council Bluffs lead information guide reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Council Bluffs Water Works publishes a public notice for an SRF-funded lead service line replacement project and ties the project to its current map lists and customer guidance.
reviewerNote: Official Council Bluffs project notice reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Ames Water & Pollution Control
claimSummary: Ames publishes a lead inventory page with a city lead service line map inventory PDF and contact information for lead questions.
reviewerNote: Official Ames lead page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Ames Water & Pollution Control
claimSummary: Ames publishes a formal 2024 lead service line notice that explains homeowner ownership to the meter and interim guidance for households with lead service risk.
reviewerNote: Official Ames notice PDF reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Ames Water & Pollution Control
claimSummary: Ames says the property owner is responsible for the service line to the meter except when replacement is coordinated through a city water main project or related city work, so the city does not publish a standing no-cost replacement promise.
reviewerNote: Official Ames service lines page reviewed manually for ownership language.

claimSummary: West Des Moines Water Works says it is updating its lead service line inventory and still had about 7000 unknown service materials as of December 2023 while it collects survey responses and map updates.
reviewerNote: Official West Des Moines survey page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: West Des Moines Water Works points customers to ownership guidance, directs service line questions through the utility customer service team, and asks residents to complete the service line survey while inventory unknowns are reduced.
reviewerNote: Official West Des Moines FAQ reviewed manually.
